queue.task: Review 4-day gap (Mar 26-30). Assess state: 1117 tests, 7 failing tests, 3 PRs open. Set priorities for today.
queue.summary: Reviewed 4-day gap. Monkey-lang: 1117 tests (7 failing), 3 PRs open. Priorities: fix tests, check PRs, continue building. Queue unchanged.

queue.goal: Fix failing tests and stabilize monkey-lang
queue.summary: Root cause: functional stdlib tests call compose/pipe2/etc without loading STDLIB_SOURCE. Fix: prepend stdlib to test input.

queue.task: Fix functional stdlib tests: prepend STDLIB_SOURCE to compileAndRun calls in functional stdlib test suite
queue.summary: Fixed 7 failing functional stdlib tests by prepending STDLIB_SOURCE. 1124 tests, 0 failures.

queue.task: Housekeeping: git push, dashboard, PR check
queue.summary: Git pushed, dashboard live, 9 OpenClaw PRs still open (no new reviews), no monkey-lang PRs. Dashboard generate.cjs refreshed.

queue.task: What to build next — new monkey features, new project, or open source?
queue.summary: Direction: WASM compilation backend for monkey-lang. Completes the backend story (eval→VM→JIT→transpiler→WASM). Technically interesting, great for the playground.

queue.goal: Next monkey-lang milestone or new project direction
queue.summary: Planned WASM backend: 5 BUILD tasks covering binary encoder, core compiler, tests, strings/arrays, playground integration.

queue.task: WASM binary encoder: module builder with sections (type, function, export, code, memory, data)
queue.summary: WASM binary encoder complete: module builder with type/import/function/memory/global/export/code/data sections. FuncBodyBuilder fluent API. 18 tests all passing (LEB128, constants, arithmetic, locals, if/else, loops, calls, memory, globals, data, imports, fibonacci).

queue.task: WASM compiler core: AST→WASM for integers, arithmetic, comparisons, let bindings, if/else, while loops, functions
queue.summary: WASM compiler core complete. AST→WASM for integers, arithmetic, comparisons, let bindings, if/else, while/for loops, functions (including recursion), return, logical operators. 59 tests all passing.

queue.task: WASM compiler tests: verify numeric programs produce correct results via WebAssembly.instantiate
queue.summary: Tests already written in T14 — 59 tests covering all numeric programs via WebAssembly.instantiate. Fibonacci, factorial, GCD, power, nested loops all verified.

queue.task: WASM strings + arrays: linear memory layout, alloc/free, string builtins, array indexing
queue.summary: WASM strings+arrays complete. Bump allocator, array literals/indexing/len/push, string literals with data segments, 73 compiler tests (14 new). 91 total WASM tests.

queue.task: Integrate WASM backend into playground with execution mode toggle
queue.summary: WASM backend integrated into playground. Engine toggle (JIT/VM/WASM), smart result display for strings/arrays, benchmark includes WASM timing, WASM Fibonacci example. Pushed to GitHub Pages.

queue.task: Mid-session housekeeping
queue.summary: Git pushed workspace + github pages. Full test suite: 1215 passing, 0 failing, 3 skipped (up from 1124). All WASM tests green.

queue.task: Reflect on progress, assess quality
queue.summary: Reflected: WASM backend solid for numeric/array programs. Next priorities: (1) Add puts to WASM via JS imports for visible output, (2) Blog post about the WASM backend. 1215 tests, 5 backends.
Planned: 3 tasks — WASM puts/str via imports, blog post about WASM backend, TASKS.md update.

queue.task: Add puts/str to WASM backend via JS import functions — output integers, strings, arrays
queue.summary: WASM puts/str via JS imports complete. puts() calls through to formatWasmValue for smart formatting of ints/strings/arrays. 8 new tests (81 total WASM compiler tests). Playground updated and pushed.

queue.task: Write blog post: Compiling Monkey to WebAssembly
queue.summary: Blog post published: Compiling Monkey to WebAssembly. Covers binary encoder, compilation strategy, value representation, JS imports, performance numbers, test coverage. Pushed to GitHub Pages.

queue.task: Update TASKS.md with WASM backend progress and next steps
queue.summary: TASKS.md updated: WASM backend added to project description, today section with full progress, WASM backlog items (closures, string concat, hash maps, GC).

queue.task: Research topic TBD based on morning direction
queue.summary: Explored WasmGC: standardized in Wasm 3.0 (Sep 2025), baseline in all browsers. Key insight: could target WasmGC structs/arrays for Monkey values instead of linear memory + custom GC. Captured closure strategies and next steps in scratch note.

queue.task: End-of-session housekeeping
queue.summary: Skipped — only 30min in, 4.5h remaining. Will do end-of-session housekeeping at boundary.

queue.task: Decide next project/feature: WASM closures, string concat, or new exploration
queue.summary: Direction: (1) WASM string operations (concat, comparison, str() builtin), (2) Update monkey-lang README for 5-backend showcase, (3) Additional WASM compiler features if time remains.

queue.goal: Plan: WASM string operations and README update
queue.summary: Planned 6 BUILD tasks: string concat, str() builtin, string comparison, tests, README update, final push.

queue.task: WASM string concat: implement __str_concat(ptr1, ptr2) → new_ptr, hook up + operator for strings
queue.summary: String concat implemented: __str_concat via JS import, type inference for + operator with strings. 9 new tests.

queue.task: WASM str() builtin: convert integers to string representation in linear memory
queue.summary: Implemented in T22 — str() converts integers via formatWasmValue, writes back to WASM memory via writeString helper.

queue.task: WASM string comparison: implement == and != for strings
queue.summary: Implemented in T22 — __str_eq import, type inference for == and != with strings.

queue.task: Add WASM-specific tests for string operations (concat, str, comparison)
queue.summary: 9 tests added in T22 — concat, str(), comparison, loops, fibonacci output. 90 total WASM compiler tests.

queue.task: Update monkey-lang README: 5 backends, 1200+ tests, architecture diagram, WASM section
queue.summary: README updated: 5 backends in subtitle, WASM blog post link, architecture diagram with all 5 backends, WASM in feature comparison, 1235 test count.

queue.task: Push final state, update playground bundle, git push all repos
queue.summary: All repos pushed: workspace, monkey-lang, github.io. Playground bundle updated with string operations. All 3 repos in sync.

queue.task: Decide next: WASM closures, new project, or deepen existing work
queue.summary: Direction: WASM closures using function tables and call_indirect. Will represent closures as (env_ptr, table_index) pairs. Makes the WASM backend support first-class functions.
Planned 5 tasks for WASM closures: table support in encoder, closure representation, anonymous function compilation, tests, housekeeping.

queue.task: Add WASM table support to binary encoder (table section, element section, call_indirect)
queue.summary: Table support added to encoder: addTable, addElement, callIndirect. Test passes for dispatch via function table.

queue.task: Implement closure representation: heap-allocated (table_index, env_ptr) pairs in WASM compiler
queue.summary: Closure representation implemented: TAG_CLOSURE heap objects, environment structs, call_indirect dispatch. Free variable analysis. All 90 existing tests pass.

queue.task: Compile anonymous functions and closures to table entries with environment capture
queue.summary: Already implemented in T31 — anonymous functions compiled as WASM funcs with env_ptr, registered in function table.

queue.task: Test WASM closures: makeAdder, counter, map/filter with closures
queue.summary: WASM closure tests: 9 new tests all passing (makeAdder, factory, multi-capture, higher-order, IIFE). Fixed section ordering, free variable analysis, named function wrapping. 99 total WASM compiler tests.

queue.task: Mid-session: git commit, push, update daily log
queue.summary: Git pushed all repos. Playground bundle updated with closures + string operations. Daily log updated.

queue.task: Plan next work: what else can be done in the remaining ~4 hours
queue.summary: Direction: Build a comprehensive benchmark suite comparing all 5 backends (eval, VM, JIT, transpiler, WASM) on the same programs. Add benchmark script and results to repo.
Planned 3 tasks: benchmark script, run and capture results, add to README/blog.

queue.task: Build 5-backend benchmark script: eval, VM, JIT, transpiler, WASM — for fibonacci, sum, factorial, GCD, nested loops
queue.summary: 5-backend benchmark built and run. WASM: 110x vs VM, 52x vs JIT. Transpiler also fast (V8 optimized). 12 benchmarks across recursive, loops, closures, branching. WASM wins 9/12.

queue.task: Run benchmarks, capture results, format as markdown table
queue.summary: Benchmark results formatted as markdown table. WASM 110x vs VM, 52x vs JIT. Wins 9/12 benchmarks.

queue.task: Add benchmark results to README and blog
queue.summary: Benchmark results added to README (5-backend table) and blog post (updated numbers, closures, 108 tests). All repos pushed.

queue.task: Decide next: WASM hash maps, string concat in WASM memory, or start new project
queue.summary: Direction: Check OpenClaw PRs for review comments. Then pick between WASM string concat in linear memory or a new mini-project.

queue.task: Check OpenClaw PRs for review comments or needed updates
queue.summary: 9 OpenClaw PRs checked — no human reviews, only automated bot comments. Nothing actionable. PRs need maintainer attention.

queue.task: Plan next project/feature based on PR status
queue.summary: Direction: Add WASM and transpiler modes to REPL, plus :bench command for in-REPL benchmarking. Update version info to reflect 5 backends and 1245 tests.

queue.task: Add WASM + transpiler engine modes to REPL and :bench command
queue.summary: REPL v0.3.0: 5 engine modes (eval/vm/jit/wasm/transpiler). :engine wasm/:engine transpiler. :bench compares all 4 backends. Async WASM execution. --wasm/--transpiler CLI flags.

queue.task: Add monkey compile command: monkey --compile file.monkey → file.wasm
queue.summary: monkey --compile: compiles .monkey → .wasm binaries. Example: fib.monkey → fib.wasm (353 bytes, standalone). Prints Node.js run command.

queue.task: Add --run-wasm flag and more example programs for WASM showcase
queue.summary: WASM file execution (monkey --wasm file.monkey) + 3 example programs: collatz.monkey, closures.monkey, fib.monkey. All run correctly via WASM with string output.

queue.task: Think: what new project to start with remaining 4 hours
queue.summary: Direction: Build a WASM disassembler (wasm2wat) in JavaScript. Takes .wasm binaries → readable WAT text. Inverse of the encoder built today. Demonstrates deep binary format understanding.
Planned 6 tasks: binary reader, section decoders, code section, WAT formatter, tests, CLI integration.

queue.task: Create project structure and binary reader: LEB128 decoder, section parser, module header
queue.summary: WASM disassembler built: BinaryReader (LEB128, floats, strings), WasmDisassembler (all sections), WAT formatter. Successfully disassembles monkey-lang .wasm output to readable WAT.

queue.task: Decode type, import, function, memory, global, export, table sections
queue.summary: Implemented in T48.

queue.task: Decode code section: function bodies, locals, instruction stream with operands
queue.summary: Implemented in T48.

queue.task: WAT text formatter: pretty-print module as valid S-expression WAT
queue.summary: Implemented in T48.

queue.task: Test against monkey-lang .wasm output + manual test cases
queue.summary: 18 disassembler tests passing. Round-trip tests: build → binary → disassemble → WAT. Tests cover LEB128, all section types, monkey WASM output (fibonacci, closures, strings).

queue.task: CLI: wasm-dis file.wasm → file.wat, add to monkey-lang as --disassemble flag
queue.summary: CLI integration: --disassemble/--dis flag. Works with both .wasm binaries and .monkey source files (compile + disassemble).

queue.task: Add :dis REPL command and :transpile command for interactive inspection
queue.summary: Added :dis and :transpile/:js REPL commands. :dis shows WASM WAT, :js shows transpiled JavaScript.

queue.task: Add WAT disassembly panel to playground (show compiled WASM alongside output)
queue.summary: Playground WAT panel: Show WAT button shows WASM disassembly, auto-shows on WASM run. Bundle updated with disassembler. Pushed to GitHub Pages.
